text: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Several graduates and students of Amirkabir University have been killed in US‑Israeli strikes, the university announced. Among the dead are Ali Nazem (master’s student), Erfan Sadeghi (mechanical engineering graduate), Mojdeh Zandi (electrical engineering graduate), Dr Saeed Shamghadri (biomedical and electrical engineering graduate), Mehdi Abbasi (power engineering graduate), Mohammad Mehdi Sharifi Jazhe (former aerospace student), Seyed Saeed Najafi (civil engineering graduate) and Mohammad Amin Zohrabi (chemical engineering graduate). @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 The Iranian army said its drones struck targets at Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport, including the new control tower, the Terminal 1 and Terminal 2 control towers, navigation systems, and airport antennas and radars. The operation aimed to disrupt military air traffic control, prevent the direction of U.S. and Israeli fighter jets, and blind surveillance and electronic warfare systems. The army vowed that revenge for "Iranian martyrs" will continue without pause. @wfwitness

France has recently pressed Bahrain to soften the language of a draft UN Security Council resolution on Hormuz. A UN discussion scheduled for today has been postponed and is likely to take place early next week. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇫🇷 🇮🇱 French Defence Minister Catherine Vautrin stated that France does not deliver weapons to Israel. She said Paris authorises on a case‑by‑case basis the sale of components for defensive systems that protect civilians, such as the Iron Dome, and for equipment strictly intended for re‑export, some of which is needed for French forces. @wfwitness

Renowned Political Economist Prof. Carlos Lopes Africa is part of the war somehow. South Africa took Israel to ICC along with others. Netanyahu is currently under court mandate. Djibouti hosts multiple military bases, with access to Bab el Mandeb Strait, the alternative to Hormuz. The entire region is seen as very important strategically by UAE, Saudi, Israel. That's why we see so much volatility in the horn. Diego Garcia in Mauritius was used for the war, now contested by President Trump. Africa has a number of dependencies from that region that will be exacerbated in the war. FULL INTERVIEW

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 The IRGC announced the 95th wave of attacks, striking U.S. HIMARS launchers on Kuwait’s Bubiyan island, a Patriot system in northern Bahrain, a gathering of senior U.S. commanders and trainers in the UAE’s Silbah area, and an Oracle AI facility in the UAE. A commercial vessel allegedly linked to Israel, the MSC Ishika, was hit in Bahrain’s Khalifa bin Salman port. Israeli cities including Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan and Kiryat Shmona were also targeted by the cluster bombs @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇵🇰 🇹🇷 🇪🇬 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 AP : Mediators from Pakistan, Turkey and Egypt are working on a compromise to bring the US and Iran back to the negotiating table, according to regional officials. The plan includes a cessation of hostilities for a certain period to allow for a diplomatic settlement, potentially in Pakistan. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reiterated Tehran’s willingness to restart talks but said it seeks a conclusive and lasting end to the conflict. He also discussed the latest developments with Turkey’s foreign minister. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇦🇹 🇮🇷 Austrian Foreign Minister Beate Meinl‑Reisinger said she stressed to her Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi the need to halt strikes on neighbouring countries and restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. She also voiced Austria’s support for a new deal on Iran’s nuclear programme and full cooperation with the IAEA. The two foreign ministers discussed regional and international developments in a phone call. @wfwitness

In the incident, three UN personnel were injured, two of them severely The IDF said a review of the launch…" ⚡️ 🇮🇩 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇺🇳 Indonesia blamed Israel for an explosion that injured three Indonesian peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, the third similar incident in a week. Indonesia’s foreign ministry said Israel’s continued military operations in southern Lebanon, including stated intentions to maintain a presence, risk destabilising the area and placing UN peacekeepers in sustained danger. The Israeli military said a review of the launch trajectory clearly indicates the rocket was fired by Hezbollah. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 💥 🇺🇸 / 🇮🇱 : IRGC Public Relations, Wave 95 of True Promise 4: ➡️ "A large-scale, multi-domain operation by the IRGC Naval and Aerospace forces was executed this morning across the broader West Asia region and the occupied Palestinian territories — in Wave 95 — with trust in Almighty God, under the code name "Ya Hassan ibn Ali (peace be upon him)", dedicated to Bibi Maryam, Ali Mardan Khan Bakhtiari, and all the brave men of Lor — using Haj Qasem, Khaybar Shekan , and Qadr missiles . ➡️ The following targets were struck and destroyed: 💬 US Army HIMARS systems on Bubiyan Island, Kuwait 💬 A US Patriot air defense system in northern Bahrain 💬 Assembly points of US MLRS/rocket artillery crews 💬 Assembly points of senior US commanders and military advisors in the "Seilbaneh" area, UAE 💬 Oracle Corporation AI technology facility (American) in the UAE ➡️ A commercial vessel named MCS Ishika , owned by the Zionist regime and sailing under a third-party flag, was struck by powerful IRGC Naval projectiles at Khalifa bin Salman Port, Bahrain. ➡️ Additionally, the following locations in occupied Palestine were subjected to heavy and sustained strikes using multi-warhead Qadr missiles: Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan , and Kiryat Shmona . ➡️ This wave is ongoing. Reports of offensive actions will be communicated to the grateful Iranian nation. " 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

The UAE says it intercepted 23 ballistic missiles and 56 drones from Iran today (April 4). Since this round of attacks began in late February, the UAE says it has intercepted 498 ballistic missiles, 23 cruise missiles, and 2,141 drones.

🇮🇹 🇶🇦 Qatar and Italy Push for Regional Stability and Energy Cooperation. During a high-level summit in Doha today, Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni held talks centered on easing global tensions and safeguarding energy supplies. The two leaders engaged in a comprehensive review of the current international landscape, emphasizing a shared commitment to de-escalation and the reinforcement of energy security frameworks. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Two U.S. HC-130J Combat King II rescue tankers departed Stuttgart Army Airfield and are heading toward the Middle East, strongly indicating the search for the missing F-15E crew member is still active and likely expanding. The HC-130J is specifically used to support combat search-and-rescue, especially by refueling HH-60/Pave Hawk rescue helicopters in flight.

Iran’s state media says fishermen found wreckage of a downed U.S. MQ-9 Reaper near Bushehr, but OSINT analysts say it’s almost certainly not an MQ-9. The debris appears to match a Chinese-made Wing Loong II, likely operated by a regional state such as Saudi Arabia or the UAE. This is at least the second Wing Loong II wreckage found by Iranian forces. Either Saudi Arabia or UAE is actively involved in operations against Iran along with U.S. and Israel. Source: fab_hinz

Visioner 👀 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Foreign Intelligence Service: ❗️ A large-scale collapse has begun in the infrastructure of data centers in Russia: outdated equipment is massively failing, and due to sanctions and the costs of the war with Ukraine, the Russian Federation is practically unable to replace it in a timely and complete manner. 👉 Over the past 6 months, the number of failures has significantly increased in small commercial data centers (processing data centers) and local IT sites of companies. 🔸 According to market participants, the problem has already affected about 20% of commercial data centers. 📌 The same situation applies to fiber-optic networks laid in the early 2000s. The warranty period for the main cables in the "West-East" direction expired in 2025. tweet

Nuno Felix RT @Mpolymer : On bashing the French and the British…a kind “Go pound sand” to the Fox News crowd who seems to have found their new talking point…so a quick note right here… that in my old world, both countries have been with us time and again. Actual professionals who have been in conflict-not DC creatures who were press secretaries like Ari-know the real deal. For example -the French military provided critical assistance to US SOF during a deadly ambush in Niger in 2017. Saved lives. In extremis. Huge appreciation from the US IC and SOF world. We know when friends come thru. Overall French CT efforts in Africa-who had the lead over us for many years- also helped save US lives. And don’t forget 90 French military were KIA in Afghanistan. That’s a pretty solid ally, when we were in a pinch. Next-US and British forces and intel r tied at the hip, but let’s focus in Afghanistan and Iraq. With 600 plus British KIA in both conflicts. 600 plus. This is all real sacrifice. British intel also on daily basis helps us wrap up terrorist networks, stop proliferation of WMD, better u detest and Russia, Iran, N Korea, and China, so many other massive value added they provide. A superb ally. Essential even. Finally, as Jonah notes, Trump is totally responsible for poisoning the alliance. European reluctance to assist is largely because Trump is so toxic it is politically impossible for other democracies-whose citizens loathe Trump to the core-to do anything other than give us the finger. So all said, as a former ntl security professional who got my hands dirty in our global escapades alongside these allies, I say thank u to them. Particularly!! the British and the French. Have a lovely day. I agree with some of this directionally, but my God the refusal to acknowledge that Trump has worked assiduously to poison the alliance and heap all the blame on Europe is just water-carrying hackery. Saying "NATO will never be the same, and Western European weakness and acquiescence is the cause" is like writing a movie review when you missed the first half of the movie. - Jonah Goldberg tweet
